title: scurrula malifolia
intro: Scurrula malifolia, also known as the poison tree or poison nut, is a plant species that belongs to the family Santalaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and preventing infections. The herbal preparations of Scurrula malifolia include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
